results.title: How Scribble Network Gets Creator Data Without APIs or Scraping
results.content:
### Path 2: Scraping

The alternative most companies turn to. Pull data directly from platforms without permission.

Scraping requires session handling, cookie storage, and accessing data without user consent. GDPR and similar privacy regulations have made this a liability minefield. Beyond legality, scraped data is unreliable. Platforms actively fight scrapers, and one update can break months of development work overnight.

Neither option works for what Scribble needed.

## How does Reclaim solve this?

Here's the insight: the problem isn't that the data doesn't exist. It's that platforms gatekeep access to it. [...] But the creator can see their own data. They can log into Instagram and see their audience demographics. They can open YouTube Studio and see their watch time analytics. The data is right there on their screen.

What if the creator could just share that data directly?

That's exactly what Reclaim enables.

Instead of Scribble requesting data from platforms (APIs) or taking it without permission (scraping), creators share their own data. They log into their social platform, see exactly what will be shared, and explicitly consent. Reclaim generates a cryptographic proof of that data that's verifiable and tamper-proof.

The creator owns their data and chooses to share it. That's it.

## What data can Scribble now access? [...] ## What data can Scribble now access?

| Platform | Data Accessible |
 --- |
| X / Twitter | Follower demographics, engagement analytics, impression data, audience interests |
| Instagram | Audience age/gender breakdown, reach metrics, story views, geographic distribution |
| YouTube | Watch time analytics, subscriber demographics, unique views, retention curves |
| TikTok | Video performance, audience demographics, trending metrics, follower growth |
| LinkedIn | Professional demographics, industry breakdown, engagement by job title |

Getting this through official API programs would cost six figures annually per platform, if they even allowed access. With Reclaim, Scribble gets it through direct creator consent.

## How does it work technically?

results.title: Scribble - LinkedIn
results.content: Scribble Network is a global community of creators, researchers, and storytellers working together to make complex ideas more accessible through high-quality content. Operating at the intersection of advertising and emerging technology, we collaborate with tech, fintech, and internet-native brands to turn products, protocols, and digital trends into clear, engaging narratives. Instead of traditional influencer marketing, Scribble Network runs structured creator campaigns that reward clarity, originality, and depth — not just audience size. For creators, Scribble Network offers transparent earning opportunities, thoughtful briefs, and a peer community focused on craft, learning, and shared growth. For brands, it’s a way to work with informed creators who can explain, contextualize, and [...] with informed creators who can explain, contextualize, and humanize their products with credibility. At its core, Scribble Network aligns incentives between builders and storytellers. Fostering a community where better understanding leads to stronger ecosystems. [...] important. Read the full guide below.  [Likes: 1, Comments: 1]; Scribble (1w): A strong product is not enough if AI search cannot find it. That was the problem RocketX came in with. RocketX had a clear differentiator: a cross-chain aggregator combining CEX and DEX liquidity in one interface. But across high-intent prompts on ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Perplexity, and Copilot, that positioning was barely showing up. So we ran a 15-day GEO sprint. Through Scribble Network, 162 independent creators published 358 pieces of content across X, Substack, Medium, Reddit, and Paragraph. The goal was simple: Make RocketX’s hybrid CEX + DEX model impossible to miss inside AI search. The results: • 9% AI Share of Voice from near-zero • 123 tracked queries covered • 38 citations and 113 mentions •

Your citation score measures how often AI assistants reference your brand when users ask questions in your category. You improve it by increasing the volume, quality, and reach of indexed content about your brand, which is exactly what a Scribble campaign does, month on month.

Scribble Network

Scribble Network

@hazy0 We're going the smart contract route for this. We will write scribbler <> citation data on chain with the copy of the answer and the engine, with a timestamp on a public blockchain. This will trigger a USDC payment instantly as the conditions are met. All logs will be viewable on a block explorer - we'll obviously make it a bit more readable. And yes - this is useful for creators as it stands now. But over time this data graph builds, and we could potentially have an industry <> Creator citation probability that is verifiable. Brands might really like that. Thinking along these lines at the moment.

Hazy [...] Scribble Network
